projectDescription: A local REST and MCP server that links to a personal WhatsApp account via Linked Devices and exposes message sending, chat listing, and message reading through HTTP API calls.
keyFeatures: WhatsApp Linked Devices pairing via numeric phone code (no QR scan required in headless use)
Persistent local session stored in .wwebjs_auth — no re-authentication across restarts
REST API for sending messages, listing chats, and reading message history
MCP-over-HTTP endpoint compatible with AI agent tooling
QR code page at /qr for browser-based authentication fallback
Configurable via environment variables (host, port, auth dir, Chrome path, headless mode)
CORS-enabled responses for local tool and browser integration
publicApiSurface: GET /health — server health and WhatsApp status
GET /api/status — detailed WhatsApp Web client state
POST /api/pairing-code — request a numeric Linked Devices pairing code
POST /api/send — send a text message to a phone number
GET /api/chats — list recent chats (query param: limit)
GET /api/messages — read messages from a chat (query params: phone_number, limit)
POST /api/messages — read messages from a chat (JSON body)
POST /api/logout — logout and destroy the current WhatsApp session
GET /qr — browser page showing the QR code for authentication
